entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Plus - Pune
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce that we’re launching a series of quarterly SAP Stammtisch Plus gatherings, beginning with our first meetup on March 14th, 2026.

This event is an excellent opportunity for SAP professionals, enthusiasts, and anyone interested in learning more about SAP technologies to come together, learn, network with like-minded individuals, and have fun.

Are you wondering what an SAP Stammtisch Plus event is?

SAP Stammtisch Plus is a local grassroots community event where SAP Community members come together to share knowledge about SAP-related topics. The event's primary focus is networking & discussions, with 2 or 3 sessions from community members.

Learn more about it at: SAP Stammtisch - what is it about?

What is in it for you?

Let’s cut to the chase, what will you get by attending or contributing to this event?

1. Networking – You get to meet like-minded tech folks and have more space for networking.
2. Learning – You will learn about the latest SAP technologies from experts.

Theme

Decoding Business AI: From Embedded Intelligence to Enterprise Impact

When?
March 14th, 2026, from 9:30 AM till 12:30 PM. Registration starts at 9:00 AM.

Where?
PRIMUS HQ, Survey No 34/3, Plot No 4,5,6, Rohan Seher Lane,
Pan Card Club Rd, Baner, Pune, Maharashtra - 411045

Registration

Please register using this link: https://events.primustechsys.com/stammtisch2026 

Note: We have only limited seats available, so register ONLY if you plan to attend IN-PERSON on March 14, Saturday in Pune.

We hope to see you all there and look forward to an engaging and productive event. Don't forget to register using the link provided above to secure your spot. See you soon!

Best Regards,
SAP Community Group – Pune

entry.title: Re: Is there enhancement to stop creating inspection lot or make it "skipped" in case of r
entry.content:
Hello MeenakshiG, 
first of all, thanks for your response.

I'd asked the QM functional consultant about the first option and second one, weren't suitable for him.

I started to search more about post-process concept but found nothing, could you tell me more about it or refer any resource for it? 

I had tried to find another way, so I used implicit enhancement at the end of "QPL1_INSPECTION_LOT_CREATE" and I changed the lot field (QALS-KZSKIPLOT) = 'X' which means skipped, but the lot status still the same even its field (KZSKIPLOT = 'X') skipped in QALS record.

entry.title: BI Remediation Checks during ECC to S/4HANA Migration
entry.content:
As SAP prepares to end support for ECC by 2027, many organizations are making the crucial move to SAP S/4HANA. The shift can unlock improved capabilities and performance, but it can also introduce changes that ripple through the BI landscape if we don’t plan for them early.

From a BI perspective, there are multiple aspects to consider during the transition. This blog focuses on the BI remediation checks that help protect data trust, reporting, and decision-making continuity during and after the migration.

Why do we need BI remediation? 
The migration from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA introduces significant changes to the underlying data structures. These changes impact how data is extracted, modelled, and consumed in the BI system. Because of that, reporting remediation becomes critical to ensure data consistency, accuracy, and continuity in analytics after the migration.

With remediation, we ensure:

  • BI models and reports remain functional and aligned with the new S/4HANA structures
  • Disruption is minimized for business operations and reporting processes

Data model simplifications in S/4HANA (what changes underneath)
When SAP S/4HANA was first introduced, the SAP ERP 6.0 data model underwent major restructuring. Redundancies were removed, and many smaller tables were consolidated into fewer, larger ones.
Figure 1 can help visualize the concept and why “what worked in ECC” may not behave the same way in S/4HANA.

                                                                                                 FIGURE 1

Impact of S/4HANA migration on the BI system (what to check)

1) Impact on BW Datasources / Extractors
If you run SAP BW, one of the first questions is:
“After ECC becomes S/4HANA, will my extractors and DataSources still be supported and behave the same way?”
SAP note 2500202  provides an Excel sheet produced by SAP that details the impact of S/4HANA on BW Datasources. This sheet is periodically updated. The same note also contains a PPT that guides us on how to interpret and use the Excel list.

A proven approach is:

  • Inventory your active extractors (e.g., via ROOSOURCE).  
  • Identify which are truly used (process chains / BI statistics where available)
  • Cross-check them against the SAP note 2500202 list and categorize outcomes (no change / restricted / obsolete / alternative required)

2) Tables with Replacement Objects (don’t rely on “classic tables” blindly)
If, as part of your BI remediation strategy, you plan to continue using certain classic ECC tables, for example, relying on MARD instead of adopting the newer MATDOC structure, you must depend on the corresponding Replacement Object rather than accessing the table directly.
With the data model simplification in SAP S/4HANA, many traditional ECC tables are no longer the authoritative “single source of truth.” In many cases, data is aggregated or derived via CDS views that join and enrich information from multiple sources. Because of this shift, it becomes essential to transition from reading classic tables (such as MARC, MARD, and others) to using their designated Replacement Objects, ensuring that analytics and integrations receive accurate, up‑to‑date data.

  • In Native HANA, it is advisable to verify whether a table has a replacement object or whether it can still be used directly.
  • In SAP BW, the datasource typically absorbs most of the underlying data model changes. However, in hybrid scenarios, particularly when calculation views are built on ECC base tables or when custom datasources built on ECC base tables, it becomes essential to thoroughly assess the impact on the affected tables.

Figure 2 shows an example for table MARD. NSDM_E_MARD serves as the replacement view for the classic MARD table. Although data continues to reside in MARD, those entries generally represent older documents migrated to S/4HANA or new S/4HANA records that are stored without measure fields. To obtain the full set of information (both characteristics and measures) for transactions created in S/4HANA, you must use the replacement object, in this case NSDM_E_MARD, rather than relying on MARD directly.

                                                                                                     FIGURE 2

3) Replacement of obsolete tables
Some ECC tables are replaced or made obsolete, and direct reporting on such tables is no longer recommended or supported. Examples tables include:

  • KONV, VBUP, VBUK, EIKP, EIPO

Obsolete tables may still exist, but they can behave as compatibility views. For accuracy and performance, they should be switched to transparent tables where applicable.

  • SAP Note 3250204: describes about the replacement table PRDC_ELEMENTS for KNOV table.
  • SAP Note 2198647: talks about tables VBUP, VBUK and SD data model changes.
  • SAP Note 3482527: covers about new S/4HANA foreign trade. 

4) Key fields changed (primary key shifts can break pipelines)
In S/4HANA, the primary keys of some tables have changed compared to ECC. Some examples are:

  • VBFA
  • FAAV_ANLC

These changes directly affect how data is stored, identified, and retrieved. That can break:

  • Joins in HANA models
  • Deduplication logic
  • Delta logic or key-based transformations in BW

Action

  • Review where these tables are used in extraction/transformation layers
  • Re-check join conditions, uniqueness assumptions, and surrogate key logic
  • Re-test deltas and aggregations end-to-end

5) Field length extensions (small change, big downstream effect)
Some fields were extended in S/4HANA to support broader functional requirements, enhanced integration needs, and future extensibility.
One such example is:

  • VBAP-VBTYP (Char1) Document Category replaced by VBAP-VBTYPL (Char4)
    • SAP Note 2495386 details the BW datasource that uses this object.

Another major example:

  • Material Number (SAP Note 2215424) changed from char(18) to char(40)

A key design decision regarding material numbers is whether your organization will:

  • Adopt a true 40‑character material number, introducing a new business meaning, or
  • Retain the existing 18‑character logic, while simply expanding the technical field length to 40 characters.

The chosen approach directly influences how material numbers flow through your BI data models and determines the adjustments required in downstream reporting and integrations.

6) Centralized Master Data via Business Partner (Customer/Vendor integration)
SAP fundamentally changed how master data is managed by introducing a unified, centralized model for customers, vendors, and business partners. This replaces the traditional ECC siloed structures with a harmonized object: the Business Partner (BP).
It’s not a “technical-only” change, it’s a semantic change in how master data is governed
Please check the SAP Note 2265093 note for more information.

7) Redirecting ECC Virtual Tables in HANA Calculation Views
When your BI models use calculation views that reference ECC base tables, these views must be updated after the migration to ensure they point to the corresponding objects in the S/4HANA system. Redirecting them is essential to prevent calculation view failures and to avoid inconsistent or incorrect analytical results after the migration. Please refer the blog for mitigation of this impact.

 
Final thoughts: ECC to S/4HANA migration is not only an ERP transformation, but also a BI continuity challenge. The safest approach is to treat BI remediation as a structured workstream, validating extractors, replacing obsolete dependencies, and proactively addressing table/key/field changes before they surface as production reporting issues.

If this post helps you structure your BI remediation checks, please consider sharing your own experiences or additions in the comments.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: What’s New as of 2026-03-02
entry.content:
Joule | AI Enhancements


Several improvements have been introduced to make working with Joule more flexible, intuitive, and user-friendly, with a strong focus on giving users more control.

 

What’s improved:


• Use Joule Without Saving a Report: You can now start using Joule immediately without first saving a report, allowing you to gain insights faster directly from your current context.

• Ask Joule About Charts: Joule can now understand the data behind charts, allowing you to ask questions about trends, comparisons, or calculated values shown in a visualization.

• Stop an Ongoing Joule Response: Users can now interrupt Joule while it is generating and streaming a response, providing more control over the interaction.


• Reasoning AI Model: From the Settings screen, you can now select a specific Reasoning AI Model for complex questions, while the Default AI Model handles faster tasks like chat title generation.


For an overview of supported and recommended AI models, see Supported Foundation Models.

 

Process Management | Download and Upload of Process Instances


We have enhanced the XLSX download and upload experience to improve operational efficiency for scenarios like daily closing chains.


• Consolidated XLSX Download: Downloading multiple selected process instances now produces one consolidated XLSX file instead of a ZIP containing multiple files.

• Source Process Mapping: The downloaded file includes a new “Source Process” column that stores the originating process ID for each instance.


• Flexible Uploads: The Upload action is now available even when no process is selected. If the XLSX contains the “Source Process” column, the system creates instances based on that mapping.


Why it matters


This improves operational efficiency for scenarios like daily closing chains by reducing repetitive uploads, improving consistency across templates, and lowering the chance of format-related errors.

For more information about this update, see Process Management.

 

Modeling Screen | Processing Functions


With the latest update, the new modeling function design is now the standard UI. Previously offered as an optional feature, this design moves the Input, Lookup, Signature, Output, and Checks node to the properties panel allowing you to view and update all function settings in one place.

 

 

 

For more information, see Function Building Blocks.

 

Message Logs


We also have enhanced the message logs from modeling and other areas of the application. Now, when the message log shows more than 50 lines from singular, multiple, or whole environment activation or run, then a redirection to Application Monitor will be shown.


We aim to make sure the specific Run ID is focused to ensure that users will be able to see all relevant messages from a particular action and avoid missing an important line.

 

RFA | Improved Logging Visibility


We’ve improved RFA logging visibility by enriching the results (Y-table) with additional posting messages on top of the existing Application Monitor updates.

Header-level Outcomes: Technical messages like “Document posted successfully” are now written into the technical field FS_PER_MSG_TEXT_.

 

 

Easier Tracing: This enhancement makes it easier to trace posting feedback directly on the Data Management screen (with Show technical fields enabled) without relying solely on application logs.


Built-In Help and Notifications

 

SAP Companion in Visualize: The Built-In Help, also known as SAP Companion, is now accessible within the Visualize screen to provide guided information and detailed descriptions to enhance your user experience.

 


Expanded Notifications: You can now receive both in-app and email notifications when a scheduled job run starts and completes, provided these channels are activated in your user settings.


For detailed guidance on how to subscribe to notifications, please refer to the Notifications documentation.

That’s a wrap! 🎁

Thank you for reading. We hope these updates help make your day-to-day work in SAP PaPM Cloud even smoother. Stay tuned for the next edition of our “What’s New” series!

entry.title: Enhancing Standard CDS Views in FIORI My Inbox
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Fiori My Inbox is a powerful application designed for efficient task and workflow management within the SAP ecosystem. The information about the specific work item was not sufficient for the approvers to proceed further, hence added additional details to the standard screen. This enhancement allows structured business data, often residing in the SAP HANA database, to seamlessly become part of the Fiori user experience. In this blog, we will explore how to enhance Fiori My Inbox by extending a standard Core Data Services (CDS) view.

Objective:

The primary goal is to enhance the standard Fiori My Inbox by extending the standard CDS view, ensuring a unified interface for managing and processing workflows. The journey involves several key steps, and we will walk through each of them to achieve a streamlined workflow experience.

Steps to Follow:

Step 1: Create an extended view and add Virtual element.

Begin by creating and activating a CDS view with the required annotations. Include virtual elements to access any ABAP logic inside CDS view. 

For this we have extended the standard CDS view C_PurchaseOrderFs and implemented additional fields using virtual elements concept.

Sample Code:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZC_PURCHASEORDER'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension on Standard C_PurchaseOrderFs'

extend view C_PurchaseOrderFs with ZFI_C_PURCHASEORDERFS_EXT

association [1..1] to ZFI_MY_INBOX_REQUEST_DETL_CDS as _Requester on I_PurchaseOrderEnhanced.purchaseorder = _Requester.PoNo

{
  @ui.fieldGroup: [{
    qualifier: 'Recipient',
    groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
    position: 40,
    exclude: false,
    importance: #HIGH }, {
        qualifier: 'Detail3',
        groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
        position: 40,
        exclude: false,
        importance: #HIGH
    }]
  @EndUserText.label: 'Requested By'
  _Requester.RequestedBy         as RequestedBy,

  @ui.fieldGroup: [{
    qualifier: 'Recipient',
    groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
    position: 50,
    exclude: false,
    importance: #HIGH }, {
        qualifier: 'Detail3',
        groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
        position: 50,
        exclude: false,
        importance: #HIGH
    }]
  @EndUserText.label: 'Requested Department'
  _Requester.RequestedDepartment as RequestedDepartment,

  @ui.fieldGroup: [{
  qualifier: 'Recipient',
  groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
  position: 60,
  exclude: false,
  importance: #HIGH }, {
      qualifier: 'Detail3',
      groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
      position: 60,
      exclude: false,
      importance: #HIGH
  }]
  @EndUserText.label: 'Buyer Name'
  _Requester.BuyerName           as BuyerName,

  @EndUserText.label: 'Buyer Note'
  @ObjectModel.readOnly: true
  @ObjectModel.virtualElement: true
  @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_MM_PO_BUYER_NOTE'

  @ui.fieldGroup: [{
  qualifier: 'Recipient',
  groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
  position: 70,
  exclude: false,
  importance: #HIGH }, {
      qualifier: 'Detail3',
      groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
      position: 70,
      exclude: false,
      importance: #HIGH
  }]
  cast('' as abap.char(1333))    as BuyerNote,

  @EndUserText.label: 'Supplier Participants'
  @ObjectModel.readOnly: true
  @ObjectModel.virtualElement: true
  @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_MM_PO_SUPPL_NOTE'

  @ui.fieldGroup: [{
  qualifier: 'Recipient',
  groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
  position: 80,
  exclude: false,
  importance: #HIGH }, {
      qualifier: 'Detail3',
      groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
      position: 80,
      exclude: false,
      importance: #HIGH
  }]
  cast('' as abap.char(1333))    as SupplierParticipants,

  @EndUserText.label: 'Supplier Note'
  @ObjectModel.readOnly: true
  @ObjectModel.virtualElement: true
  @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_MM_PO_SCOPE_NOTE'


  @ui.fieldGroup: [{
  qualifier: 'Recipient',
  groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
  position: 90,
  exclude: false,
  importance: #HIGH }, {
      qualifier: 'Detail3',
      groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
      position: 90,
      exclude: false,
      importance: #HIGH
  }]
  cast('' as abap.char(1333))    as ScopeWork,

  @EndUserText.label: 'Approver'
  @ObjectModel.readOnly: true
  @ObjectModel.virtualElement: true
  @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_MM_PO_APPROVERS_LIST'

  @ui.fieldGroup: [{
  qualifier: 'Recipient',
  groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
  position: 100,
  exclude: false,
  importance: #HIGH }, {
      qualifier: 'Detail3',
      groupLabel: 'Recipient', groupLabel_asOtr: 'FA163EDF73161EE587C742C45B2B0F3D',
      position: 100,
      exclude: false,
      importance: #HIGH
  }]
  cast('' as abap.char(1333))    as ApproverID1
}

We need to mention the SADL class using an annotation @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy. 

Activate the CDS View to ensure that no discrepancies to the existing code.

Step 2: Create enhancement class in ABAP.

Create mentioned enhancement class with the interface ‘IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ’.

Implement the method ‘IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ~CALCULATE’

Define annotations using the Annotation Modeler to provide additional information about the service metadata. 

Step 3: Load Metadata

Ensure the new fields are added to the metadata, load the metadata using the transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.

 Output:

The above is final output of how we modified this Fiori My Inbox, we can visualize the previous configuration and results after the enhancement.

By providing a unified and user-friendly interface like this, we can enhance efficiency and user satisfaction in handling tasks and approvals.

Before enhancement:

After enhancement:

Summary:

In conclusion, enhancing the standard CDS view in Fiori My Inbox transforms task and workflow management. The seamless interaction with business data in real-time contributes to an overall streamlined workflow experience within the organization.

By following these steps, organizations can unlock the full potential of Fiori My Inbox, optimizing their workflow processes and improving collaboration across various backend systems and applications.

entry.title: new way to create destination from BAS
entry.content:
Destination creation from BAS

Introduction : A destination in SAP BTP isa secure configuration object used to define connection details for outbound communication from BTP applications to remote services or systems, whether they are on-premise or cloud-based. They store essential technical information—such as URLs, authentication credentials, and proxy types—to enable seamless, secure integration.

Step1 : Open bas in btp 

Step2:open BAS, then you can see sidebar at left in that click on three dots then you can see service center option 

(service center in SAP system (that is connected to your trial account)) 

Step3:after clicking that you can see add system option 

Step4: provide the details of system like(system name, description, url, system type, client, proxy type, user name, password ) 

Step5: click on add then you can see message like hana2026 has been created 

Step6: come back to BTP subaccount and cross check the destination whether it is created or not 

Conclusion : Here is the new way to create destination in Business technology platform through business application studio, using service center option in business application studio we can create destination from BTP also using destination option in sap BTP sub account,

entry.title: Reference Tiles in SAP Fiori: The Smart Way to Share Apps
entry.content:
Introduction: Ever found? You've got a perfectly good Fiori app that works great for one team, but now another team wants the exact same app in their workspace, The answer is  reference tiles. A reference tile simply means creating another copy of the same tile in a different group, without impacting the original app. Think of it like sharing the same tile with multiple user groups.

For example, let’s say the tile is already available for Group 1, and they’re actively using the application. Now you want Group 2 to access the same tile as well. Instead of creating a new tile or modifying the original, you can just create a reference tile for Group 2. This way, both groups see the same app tile, and any changes to the main app remain unaffected.

In this blog, I’m using the standard app “Maintain Data Sources” to demonstrate how this works.

Benefits

  • One original app
  • Multiple "shortcuts" pointing to it
  •  Update once, works everywhere
  • No duplicate maintenance 

Note: it's not copy of tile it is a pointer of original application 

Step 1: Find Your Application
Click the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library  and search for your application.

Step 2: Copy the Business Catalog
In the configuration, copy the business catalog. In that catalog, only the tile will be there.

Step 3: Locate the Tile in Copied Catalog
Once you've copied and pasted the catalog, search within it. The tile will be there, and now you can create a reference.

Step 4: Drag and Drop for Reference
After finding the tile, you need to drag it. Two options will appear: Reference and Delete. Drop the tile on Reference.

Step 5: Select Target Catalog
Once you drop it at reference, it will ask which catalog you want to create the reference in. Select your target catalog.

Step 6: Confirmation Message
After selecting the catalog, you'll receive a "Reference Created" message as shown in the screenshot.

Step 7: Create Target Mapping Reference 
After completing the tile reference, you must create a reference for target mapping - otherwise, it won't navigate anywhere!
Go to Target Mapping and select the mapping. In the bottom toolbar, click Create Reference.

 

Step 8: Select Same Catalog for Target Mapping
After clicking Create Reference, select the same catalog as the tile. You'll receive confirmation that the reference was created in the catalog.

Step 9: Now you can verify that the tile is created in the targeted catalog and works properly.

Capture in Transport Request (TR)** - Don't forget to capture this in a TR, otherwise you can't transport it to other systems!

Conclusion:  Reference tiles are like having a clone button that doesn't create maintenance. You can share the same application across multiple user groups without the complexity of managing duplicate versions.

.

entry.title: Re: S4 BRF+ user not receiving email
entry.content:
Hello @Kashif3

You seem to have chosen the wrong tag (Public Cloud) while posting your question.

BRR+ OPD setup in that form SU01, SOST and tables like APOC_D_ITEM _E in your context are on the private cloud/onpremise.

PO email output troubleshooting is done through Output Parameter Determination app in public cloud, especially the Email Recipient determination step for PO, because recipient determination can be dependent on purchasing org, group, company code, etc.

BR

Chris

entry.title: Dynamic Closing Month in SAC (BW Import Model)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m working with a BW Query-based Import Model in SAP Analytics Cloud and I’m facing a design challenge related to time handling.

Business Requirement

We have two different visualization needs within the same story:

  1. Numeric point charts (KPI tiles)
    → Should always display values for a selected “Reporting Month” (Closing Month).

  2. Bar charts (Trend analysis)
    → Should display performance for the last 13 months relative to the selected Closing Month
    (i.e., Closing Month - 12 months up to Closing Month).

Important Constraint

The “Closing Month” is not always Current Month - 1.

Due to operational timing (e.g., delayed invoicing), the closing month is effectively the latest available month in the dataset, which may vary over time.

Additionally:

  • Users must be able to override the Closing Month at runtime
    (e.g., instead of December 2025, they may select November 2025).

  • This selection should:

    • Update KPI tiles to reflect the selected month.

    • Dynamically adjust the bar chart to show the rolling 13-month window relative to that selection (e.g., Nov 2024 – Nov 2025).

Current Approach

Currently, I am:

  • Using a Story Variable for Reporting Month.

  • Programmatically setting filters for widgets based on that variable via scripting.

This works functionally, but:

  • It feels performance-intensive.

  • It introduces additional story scripting complexity.

Question

Within the constraints of a BW Import Model, what would be the recommended architectural approach to:

  • Support a dynamic “Closing Month” selection,

  • Drive both single-month KPIs and rolling 13-month trend charts,

  • Minimize scripting and optimize performance?

Any best practices or design recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Onur

entry.title: Maintain Form Templates in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Maintain Form Templates can be used to create Custom Form Templates using standard templates in SAP S.4 HANA Public Cloud with Key User Extensibility.

Maintain Form Templates is a tile to create new Custom Form Templates in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud.

 

Maintain Form Templates in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud

  • Maintain Form Templates App views a list of all available form templates and filter the templates by their assigned languages or countries
  • With this, create a new custom form template with one original language and the option to translate it to different target languages
  • Copy an SAP-predelivered template to custom form templates for the next editing and reuse
  • Delete custom form templates that are no longer needed
  • Access older versions of the custom template, download them and compare two versions at object level
  • Custom fields can also be added to the Custom form templates and values can be mapped using custom logic.

Navigate to Maintain Form Templates App

Predelivered Templates

Choose filters to filter the templates with required templates.

This is the Predelivered template of Purchase Order

Choose Download to edit this template in Adobe Life Cycle Designer

These are the files of the form template.

Custom Templates

For the development of custom form templates, the form template file (XDP) can be downloaded to edit it with the Adobe LiveCycle Designer and upload it as custom template in the original language.

Choose New to create custom template.

Provide the Custom Template name, choose the downloaded file as template file,

choose Dats Source same as Purchase Order template Data Source and

Form Type as Content.

Custom form Template

Save the Custom Form Template

Download the Custom Form Template file to make the necessary changes and re-upload it.

Downloaded files of the Custom Form template.

Adobe LifeCycle Designer for SAP Solutions

Open Adobe LifeCycle Designer to edit the custom form

Choose the form

This is the standard form template, and this can be changeable.

Re upload the custom form after making necessary changes.

Choose Custom Form Template

Custom Form Template has been uploaded.

Assign Form Templates

Assign the custom form templates

Navigate to Assign Form Templates

Maintain an entry for the Custom Form Templates

Maintain the entry for the custom Form Templates as same as standard templates.

Choose new entries.

Custom form template has been maintained.

Output Parameter Determination

Maintain custom form template in output parameter determination

Navigate to Output Parameter Determination

Choose Purchase Order

Choose Form Template as determination

Add a entry or edit the existing entries for custom form templates.

Choose Output type as Purchase Order

Choose Channel as Print

Choose Sender as IN

Add custom form template name in form template

Check and activate the changes.

Testing Form Templates

Navigate to create purchase order and check the forms generated.

Custom form template is triggered.

This is the custom form template

Conclusion

In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud, Custom Form templates with specific document types can be created using standard form templates in Maintain form Template app.

Those Custom Form template can be download and modify the form design in Adobe Life Cycle designer and same can be uploaded to that form.

These Custom Form Template can be configured in Output Parameter determination for respective business process.

References

  • Maintain Form Templates | SAP Help Portal
  • Output Management | SAP Help Portal
  • Output Parameter Determination | SAP Help Portal
  • Assign Form Template to Output Type | SAP Help Portal
  • Assign Form Templates | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Media Handling in SAP CAPM – Backend and Frontend Implementation
entry.content:
Hi,

Introduction:

Media handling is a key requirement in enterprise applications for uploading, storing, and retrieving files like PDFs and documents. The Cloud Application Programming Model (CAPM) from SAP enables efficient media management through OData Service services. In this blog, we implement end to end media handling with backend and UI5 integration

Backend Implementation 

  • Create database schema  
  • Create service definition 
  • Implement custom logic 

Step 1: 

First, we define an entity to store file metadata and binary content 

For that create one cds file under db folder 

namespace mediahanling;

entity myMedia{
    key ID: UUID;
    filename: String;
    mediatype: String;
    content: LargeBinary @Core.MediaType : mediatype;
}

This entity stores uploaded files and their metadata 

Mediatype: MIME type of file (ex: image/png, application/pdf) 

Content (LargeBinary): Stores actual file data in binary format 

Content: LargeBinary @Core.MediaType: mediatype:  

  • Content is a media stream 
  • Media type comes from mediatype field 
  • Enables file upload/download through OData 

Step2: 

  • Now we have to expose the entity as a OData service 
  • So, create cds file under srv folder 
using { mediahanling } from '../db/schema';

service MyMediaService {
    entity MediaEntity as projection on mediahanling.myMedia;
}

Step 3: 

  • Now we will create js file under srv folder and we will implement business logic to validate uploads 
const cds= require("@sap/cds");

module.exports = cds.service.impl(function(){
    const { MediaEntity } = this.entities;
    this.before('CREATE', MediaEntity , async(req)=>{
        if(!req.data.filename){
            req.error(400, "File name required");
        }
        if(!req.data.mediatype){
            req.error(400, "Media type required");
        }
        console.log("Uploading:", req.data.filename);
    })
})

Business logic is written inside service handler. Service.js file adds custom validation logic before file is stored in the database 

Const cds = require(‘@sap/cds’)  

  • Imports CAP runtime from SAP SE 
  • Provides APIs to define services, handlers and events 
  • Required to implement custom logic in CAP Application 

Module.exports = cds.service .impl(function(){}) 

  • Registers custom logic for your service 
  • CAP automatically call this when the service starts

Const {MediaEntity} = this.entities

  • Gets the exposed in service.cds 
  • MediaEntity represents your media uppload table 
  • We will this reference to attach event handler 

As of now we have Defined Entity and exposed entity as a OData Service 

Now we will upload the PDF file, for that we need to create http file

And I have created PDF folder and under PDF folder I have PDF file

 

test.http file

###
POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/my-media/MediaEntity
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "filename": "sample.pdf",
  "mediatype": "application/pdf"
}


###
PUT http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/my-media/MediaEntity(74382f14-ff18-405e-9ce9-fcdf84317f02)/content
Content-Type: application/pdf

< ./pdf/sample.pdf

When you click on send request, this request creates new media record in our CAP Service 

It stores file details (metadata) but it will not upload the actual file content

 

  • When you click on send request it, it will create metadata, it will look like this 
  • Since we have given UUID for ID in entity, Id will create automatically

Now have to take that ID, and keep it in PUT request 

  • Then, click on send request, then it is going to upload pdf 
  • Click on MediaEntity and we can see the data 

As of now we have done backend implementation, now we will do frontend implementation 

  • For that we need to create UI5 application 

 

  • While creating UI5 app, select Data Source as a Local CAP project 
  • Then choose, our CAP project and then select OData service

Once you created UI5 app, we can see UI5 app is created under App folder 

In view I have used required controls to upload file and display PDF 

<mvc:View controllerName="mediahandling.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:unified="sap.ui.unified">
    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <unified:FileUploader id="fileUploader" width="400px" height="300px" change="onFileChange" uploadOnChange="false" >
        </unified:FileUploader>
        <Button text="Submit" press="onSumbit" ></Button>

        <ScrollContainer horizontal="true" vertical="true">
                <FlexBox class="sapUiSmallMargin" direction="Column" renderType="Div" >
                    <FlexBox direction="Row" fitContainer="true" renderType="Div">
                        <PDFViewer class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd" source="{/Source}" title="PDF" height="70vh" width="100vw" isTrustedSource="true">
                            <layoutData>
                                <FlexItemData growFactor="1"/>
                            </layoutData>
                        </PDFViewer>
			        </FlexBox>
                </FlexBox>
        </ScrollContainer>

        
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

In controller.js file I have written some logic to upload file and display the PDF in UI 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
], (Controller,MessageToast) => {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("mediahandling.controller.View1", {
        
        onInit() {
            debugger;
            this.getView().setModel(new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel({
                Source: ""
            }));

                
        },
         onFileChange: function (oEvent) {
                 debugger;
                 this._file = oEvent.getParameter("files")[0];
        },
        onSumbit:function(oEvent){
            debugger;
              var oFile = this._file;

            if (!oFile) {
                MessageToast.show("Select file first");
                return;
            }

            if (oFile.type !== "application/pdf") {
                MessageToast.show("Upload PDF only");
                return;
            }

            var that = this;
            fetch("/odata/v4/my-media/MediaEntity", {
                method: "POST",
                headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    filename: oFile.name,
                    mediatype: oFile.type
                })
            })

            .then(function (res) {

                if (!res.ok) {
                    throw new Error("Metadata creation failed");
                }

                return res.json();
            })

            .then(function (data) {

                that.mediaID = data.ID;

                return fetch("/odata/v4/my-media/MediaEntity(" + that.mediaID + ")/content", {
                    method: "PUT",
                    headers: { "Content-Type": oFile.type },
                    body: oFile
                });
            })

            .then(function (res) {

                if (!res.ok) {
                    throw new Error("File upload failed");
                }

                MessageToast.show("Upload successful");

                var sUrl = "/odata/v4/my-media/MediaEntity(" + that.mediaID + ")/content";

                that.getView().getModel().setProperty("/Source", sUrl);
            })

            .catch(function (err) {
                console.error(err);
                MessageToast.show("Upload failed — check console");
            });




        }
    });
});

Putput:

Conclusion: 

In this blog, we have implemented complete media handling by creating a CAP entity, exposing it as an OData service, and uploading files from backend and frontend. We also built a UI5 application to upload and display PDF files, providing a scalable solution for managing media in enterprise applications

entry.title: Alternative Approach to Calculate “End of RLT” in S/4HANA Public Cloud (S4HPC)
entry.content:
Many of us working on S/4HANA Public Cloud projects eventually face a requirement to calculate the End of Replenishment Lead Time (RLT). In ECC, this was simple thanks to the FM “End of RLT”, but in S4HPC, there is no equivalent API or method provided.

In this blog, I’m sharing an alternative approach we successfully used to derive the End of RLT date using standard Public Cloud capabilities—without custom logic.

The Business Use Case

We recently explored a scenario where a customer needed real-time visibility into stock availability via a Web Shop interface:

  1. Transparency: Customers must see current stock at the company site. If stock is unavailable, the system should display an estimated availability date.
  2. Sales order: During actual sales order creation, the system must only confirm quantities based on physical stock/firmed receipts. It should not confirm against RLT (to avoid promising stock that isn't physically secured yet).

Existing Configuration

To meet the "Order Integrity" requirement, the standard Scope of Check is configured to ignore the Check Horizon:

  • Settings: Considers only firmed stock (Stock in hand, confirmed POs, Production Orders).
  • Check Horizon: Set to "Ignore Check Horizon."

 

The Proposed Solution: Using the "WS" Checking Rule

To calculate the estimated availability date (End of RLT) without affecting your standard Sales Order logic, follow this two-step approach:

  1. Configuration Pre-requisite

Create a new Scope of Check by copying your existing configuration. Use the new checking rule "WS" (Web Shop), which SAP released as part of the 2602 upgrade. Unlike your standard rule, configure "WS" to consider the RLT.

 

  1. Selecting the Right ATP API

SAP provides several APIs depending on your granularity (Plant, MRP Area, Storage Location, or Batch) and license (Basic vs. Advanced ATP).

  • For standard checks, you might use checking rule "A" to mimic a Sales Order and get the confirmed quantity.
  • For the End of RLT specifically, performance is key, so we need to select the lighter API.

The "Smart" Workaround: Calculating End of RLT

To find the exact date the RLT ends without a dedicated FM, we can "force" the ATP API to look at the horizon:

  • API Recommended: API_PRODUCT_AVAILY_INFO_BASIC (This is lightweight compared to the full aATP API).
  • The Logic: Call the API using your new checking rule "WS" and pass an intentionally "infinite" quantity (e.g., 99,999).
    •  

       

  • The Result: Because the requested quantity exceeds physical stock, the system will look toward the RLT. The API will return the date on which that 99,999 units would be "available."

Technically, this date is the End of RLT, identical to what you would see in transactions MD04 or CO09.

 

Summary

By using the ATP API with a high dummy quantity and a specific RLT-enabled checking rule, we successfully replicate the ECC "End of RLT" logic in S/4HANA Cloud. This ensures your Web Shop can provide accurate "Available From" dates to customers while keeping your core Logistics execution lean and stock-based.

entry.title: Running SAP AMS Like a Product, Not a Support Factory
entry.content:
Running SAP AMS Like a Product, Not a Support Factory

Introduction

In many S/4HANA environments, the real challenge begins after go-live, during stabilisation. This blog shares a practical experience from a healthcare SAP landscape where SLA compliance was strong, yet recurring issues were eroding business confidence. I will outline what changed when we shifted focus from ticket closure to process-level stability.

A few years ago, I was supporting a large healthcare and pharmaceutical organisation in India. The SAP landscape was not small. Multiple company codes, manufacturing plants, finance, MM, SD, and integration touchpoints. At peak, the AMS team was handling close to  70 to 90 incidents per week across modules.

On paper, we were stable. SLAs were green. Response times were acceptable. Escalations were limited.

But stability is not the same as confidence.

Three weeks after one S/4HANA go-live stabilisation cycle, finance users began reporting repeated FI posting failures during month-end close. The same issue was logged again. And again. And again.

Over a two-week period, the same posting issue surfaced repeatedly, close to 8 to 10 times, mostly during month-end processing.

Each ticket was resolved within SLA. Each one was technically “closed.”
And yet, nothing was actually fixed.

From a dashboard perspective, AMS looked healthy. From a business perspective, frustration was building.

I still remember one escalation call. A senior finance stakeholder asked:

“If we are closing tickets, why does this keep coming back during month-end?”

That question was uncomfortable. Because the honest answer was ,we were optimizing for closure, not stability.

What Was Really Happening

The root cause turned out to be a configuration gap combined with a background job timing conflict. Nothing dramatic. No major design flaw. Just a gap that slipped through during hypercare.

But the way we were operating AMS made it worse.

Incidents were assigned by queue. Functional would resolve one aspect. Technical would adjust something. The ticket would close. And ownership would dissolve.

Two weeks later, month-end would expose the same weakness.

We had documentation. We had RCA notes. What we did not have was single-point accountability for eliminating recurrence.

And that is where I realised this was not a technical issue. It was an operating model issue.

The Shift

We paused low-priority enhancement work for that module. Not popular at the time.

We grouped recurring incidents instead of treating them individually. Assigned one accountable lead for the entire FI process area. Not for a ticket for the outcome.

We introduced a simple stability metric: repeat incident count per process area. Not just SLA compliance.

Repeat FI posting incidents during month-end reduced by approximately 40 to 60% over the next two stabilisation cycles..

Total weekly incident volume across SAP reduced gradually as patterns were eliminated rather than recycled.

More importantly, escalation tone changed. Instead of: “Why is this failing again?”

The conversation shifted to: ‘What risks are we proactively addressing before next month-end?’

That was a turning point.

What I Learned

For a long time, I believed strong SLA governance meant strong AMS. I defended green dashboards in meetings. In hindsight, I was defending the wrong metric.

Ticket closure is activity. Stability is outcome.

Running SAP AMS like a product means:

  • Clear ownership at process level
  • Measuring recurrence, not just response time
  • Prioritising structural fixes over cosmetic closure
  • Accepting short-term delivery pressure to gain long-term stability

It does not require heavy transformation. It requires clarity and discipline.

I have seen this pattern more than once , especially in healthcare and pharma environments where compliance and month-end accuracy are critical.

The lesson for me was simple.

If the same issue returns, the system is telling you something.
Dashboards may stay green. But business memory does not reset every month.

That experience fundamentally changed how I lead AMS today.

If you are running SAP AMS in a post-S/4HANA environment, I would be curious:

Are you measuring how fast tickets close or how rarely they return?

 

Conclusion

Stabilisation after S/4HANA go-live is not only about meeting SLAs. It is about reducing recurrence and rebuilding business confidence. By shifting focus from ticket closure to process-level accountability, we were able to improve stability in a measurable way. For organisations running SAP AMS today, the real question is not how fast incidents close, but whether the same ones continue to return.

entry.title: Re: Business role changes after upgrade in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Good day @S-O

Yes, it can still happen even if the role was transported and identical across D, T and P before the upgrade. The Upgrade Changes flag is tenant specific and reflects whether that tenant detects upgraded related deltas (catalog or restriction changes, template deltas) and whether they have already been reviewed or applied in that tenant through the post upgrade IAM tools.

You can verify this in your Manage Business Role Changes After Upgrade in each tenant and download the CSV of changes.

Then open the role in your Maintain Business Roles and compare the catalogs and restrictions you assigned, and check which catalog or restriction delta is driving the “Yes” indicator in D.

See the following  references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/b362b0a1df3a477eb577c1c71c4d43a4.html?locale=en-US 

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2868559 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model What’s New as of 2026-03-02
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

As we step into a new month, it’s time to highlight the latest updates and improvements introduced to the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud – Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM). In this edition of What’s New, I’ll guide you through the newest enhancements designed to support more efficient modeling, better transparency, and an overall smoother experience.

If you’d like to explore previous updates from the team, feel free to check out all past posts using the PaPMCloudUMWhatsNew tag.

Model

Enhanced Read Access for Protected Environments

When working in protected environments, users often notice that certain parts of the system are restricted by design. This is intentional - these environments are designed to safeguard sensitive customer data. But it also means users have limited visibility into what’s happening behind the scenes. 

When protection is turned on, several important technical details are hidden, such as: 

  • The full list of functions (active or inactive) in Manage Functions and Show Model 
  • All connections 
  • All fields 
  • Data of any internal functions 
  • The ability to run internal functions

While this protects sensitive information, it can add complexity to troubleshooting for both customers and SAP support - often requiring customer data to be exported and restored into a DEV tenant with an unprotected environment.

To improve the experience, the system was adjusted so that users with MODEL_ALL / MODEL roles  –  even with protection set to Yes – would get read access to:

  • Manage Environments

  • Manage Functions

  • Manage Fields

  • Manage Connections

Note: Sensitive items like model filters and the list of models will stay hidden. 

  • Manage Partitions

This helps users see the list of functions and the basic setup, making issue analysis much easier.

To keep the environment secure, areas that contain detailed function configuration logic remain protected and not accessible. Specifically, this refers to:  

  • Manage Model Entities 
  • Manage Model HANAs 
  • Manage Model References 
  • Manage Views and Manage Model Views 
  • Manage Joins and Manage Model Joins 
  • Manage Connections and Manage Model Connections 
  • Manage Derivations and Manage Model Derivations 
  • Manage Calculations and Manage Model Calculations 
  • Manage Allocations and Manage Model Allocations 
  • Manage Currency Conversions and Manage Model Currency Conversions 
  • Manage Unit Conversions and Manage Model Unit Conversions 
  • Manage Transfer Structures and Manage Model Transfer Structures 
  • Manage Collections and Manage Model Collections 
  • Manage Readers 
  • Manage Writers

And other areas that expose detailed function information.

Even in protected mode, users can still run functions with API-State = ACTIVE through Manage Activities, just as they do today. 

In essence, the updated behavior is designed to create a more effective balance by providing:

  • Greater transparency for troubleshooting
  • Reduced reliance on exporting data to a development tenant 
  • Continued protection of sensitive internal model information

This makes protected environments safer and more usable.

Value Help for Client Field in Conversion Functions

A new enhancement has been introduced to improve how the Client field is maintained in Conversion functions. With this update, the Client field now provides a Value Help option that allows users to select a value instead of typing it manually. 

 The Client value can now be assigned from: 

  • A field available in the input function
  • A parameter defined in the related environment 

This improvement ensures consistent client assignment and helps the system select the correct records from conversion tables based on tenant-specific data.

The enhancement is applied to the following functions: 

  • Currency Conversion 
  • Model Currency Conversion 
  • Unit Conversion 
  • Model Unit Conversion 

This change improves accuracy, reduces manual effort, and ensures consistent behavior across all conversion processes. 

That wraps up this edition!

We hope you enjoy exploring the latest features and improvements delivered in SAP PaPM Cloud UM. Don’t forget to check back next month for more updates.

Thank you for taking the time to read, and feel free to share this post with your network.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere & Google BigQuery: 3 Integration Strategies Before Zero Copy via BDC Connect

Great Post.

We have an existing SAP Global Trade Services system and are planning to integrate it with SAP Business Data Cloud.

Our objective is to bring GTS data into SAP Datasphere, combine it with other enterprise and external datasets (such as weather and cost indices), and leverage Databricks for predictive analytics and forecasting.

  • What is the recommended data movement architecture from GTS to Datasphere and further into Databricks for predictive use cases?
  • Whether data needs to be physically replicated into Databricks or can be accessed virtually?
  • How data storage is handled across Datasphere (BDC) and Databricks, and whether this results in data duplication?
  • What are the key cost components involved, specifically for:
    • BDC/Datasphere storage and processing (CU-based)
    • Databricks storage and compute (DBU-based)
    • Data movement or integration costs
    • What are the best practices to optimize storage and compute costs for such predictive scenarios?

const cds = require("@sap/cds");

const { executeHttpRequest } = require("@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client");

module.exports = cds.service.impl(async function () {

entry.title: LangGraph Checkpoint Saver for SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

LangGraph is becoming a popular framework for building AI agents that need to maintain state across interactions. If you're running these agents on SAP BTP with HANA Cloud as your database, you'll quickly find that LangGraph's built-in persistence options don't include HANA. This post introduces langgraph-checkpoint-hana, an open-source package I built to fill that gap. It implements the full LangGraph checkpointer interface for HANA Cloud, so your agent state lives in the same database as your business data.

What's a checkpointer

LangGraph is a framework for building stateful AI agents. A checkpointer saves the agent's state at each step of execution. This enables:

  • Persistent memory across sessions (the agent remembers previous interactions)
  • Fault recovery (if the process crashes, it resumes from the last saved state instead of starting over)
  • Time travel (you can inspect or roll back to any previous state)

LangGraph ships with checkpointers for PostgreSQL, SQLite, and InMemory. The community has built others for Redis, DynamoDB, Snowflake. None for HANA.

Why HANA

If you're already using HANA Vector Store for RAG (via langchain-hanadb), adding a separate database just for checkpointing adds unnecessary complexity. Vectors and agent state in the same database, same instance, same credentials.

On BTP specifically, HANA Cloud is already there. Your Kyma or CF app already has a HANA binding. Using it for checkpointing means no additional service instances.

Installation

pip install langgraph-checkpoint-hana

Requires hdbcli (installed automatically) and Python 3.10+.

Usage

Three ways to create the checkpointer:

From connection parameters

from langgraph_checkpoint_hana import HANASaver

with HANASaver.from_conn_info(
    address="your-instance.hanacloud.ondemand.com",
    port=443,
    user="DBADMIN",
    password="your-password",
) as checkpointer:
    graph = workflow.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
    result = graph.invoke(inputs, {"configurable": {"thread_id": "t1"}})

From environment variables

Useful in containers on Kyma/CF where credentials come from service bindings:

import os
os.environ["HANA_HOST"] = "your-instance.hanacloud.ondemand.com"
os.environ["HANA_PORT"] = "443"
os.environ["HANA_USER"] = "DBADMIN"
os.environ["HANA_PASSWORD"] = "your-password"

from langgraph_checkpoint_hana import HANASaver
checkpointer = HANASaver.from_env()
checkpointer.setup()

From an existing hdbcli connection

If your application already manages its own HANA connections:

from hdbcli import dbapi
from langgraph_checkpoint_hana import HANASaver

conn = dbapi.connect(address="...", port=443, user="...", password="...")
checkpointer = HANASaver(conn=conn)
checkpointer.setup()

What it creates

Calling setup() creates two tables if they don't exist:

  • LANGGRAPH_CHECKPOINTS -- stores graph state snapshots
  • LANGGRAPH_CHECKPOINT_WRITES -- stores pending writes for fault recovery

Both keyed by (thread_id, checkpoint_ns, checkpoint_id). Uses NCLOB columns for serialized data. Table existence is checked via SYS.TABLES, not try/except.

Full example with a LangGraph agent

from langgraph_checkpoint_hana import HANASaver
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, MessagesState, START, END

def chatbot(state: MessagesState) -> MessagesState:
    # your LLM call here
    return {"messages": [response]}

with HANASaver.from_env() as checkpointer:
    workflow = StateGraph(MessagesState)
    workflow.add_node("chatbot", chatbot)
    workflow.add_edge(START, "chatbot")
    workflow.add_edge("chatbot", END)

    graph = workflow.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)

    config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "session-42"}}

    # First interaction
    graph.invoke({"messages": [("human", "What's our AP aging?")]}, config)

    # Second interaction - agent remembers the first one
    graph.invoke({"messages": [("human", "Break it down by vendor")]}, config)

    # Check state
    state = graph.get_state(config)

Thread cleanup

# Delete all state for a thread
checkpointer.delete_thread("session-42")

Useful for GDPR compliance, cleaning up test data, or recovering from corrupted state (orphaned tool calls that cause downstream errors -- if you've hit this in production you know what I mean).

Technical notes

  • Writes use HANA's UPSERT ... WITH PRIMARY KEY for atomic insert-or-update
  • Serialization uses LangGraph's JsonPlusSerializer (same as the official checkpointers)
  • Async methods delegate to sync because hdbcli is a synchronous C-extension driver. For high-concurrency async deployments, wrap with asyncio.to_thread()
  • Tested with LangGraph 0.2.x/0.3.x, HANA Cloud 2024.x/2025.x, Python 3.10-3.12

Conclusion

If you're building LangGraph agents on BTP, this package lets you keep agent state in the same HANA Cloud instance where your business data already lives. No extra databases, no extra infrastructure. Install it with pip install langgraph-checkpoint-hana and try it out.

The package is MIT licensed and I welcome contributions. If you run into issues or have suggestions, open an issue on the GitHub repo.

  • PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/langgraph-checkpoint-hana/
  • GitHub: https://github.com/stubborncoder/langgraph-checkpoint-hana

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2602
entry.content:
The 2602 release of SAP Asset Performance Management introduces enhancements across Asset Data & Information Management, Asset Reliability Engineering, and Asset Health Monitoring.

This release includes improvements such as smarter notification replication between SAP ERP and APM, expanded industrial system modeling, extensible and time-aware FMEA assessments, and streamlined rule execution. Together, these updates help reduce manual effort while improving data clarity, structural transparency, and lifecycle control.

Let’s take a closer look.

Asset Data and Information Management

New Filters for Maintenance Notification Replication Between SAP ERP and APM.

A reliability engineer starts the day by reviewing maintenance notifications from SAP ERP.

The challenge

Until now, all notifications were replicated into the APM system, including urgent failures, minor inspections, test notifications, and even items irrelevant for reliability analysis. This created unnecessary noise, increased data volumes, and required manual effort to identify what truly mattered. Ensuring consistent handling across SAP ERP and the APM system added further complexity.

The solution

With enhanced filtering capabilities in the Communication System configuration, notification management becomes more intelligent and controlled.

Notification-type filters can now be defined so that only relevant notifications are replicated to the APM system. Critical maintenance issues are routed directly to reliability and performance analysis, while operational or non-relevant notifications remain in ERP.

Communication System configuration including filters for maintenance notifications

The result

  • Reliability engineers work with clean, focused notification data
  • Unnecessary data transfer between systems is reduced
  • Notifications are handled consistently across SAP ERP and the APM system

The same filtering logic also applies to APM alerts and Rules that generate notifications back to SAP ERP. Only active filters are available during notification creation and rule configuration, ensuring governed and consistent integration in both directions.

By the time trend and failure pattern analysis begins, the data is already curated — eliminating manual filtering and second-guessing.

Configuration is maintained directly within the Communication Systems settings, ensuring that the right notifications reach the right system to support efficient and reliable maintenance decisions.

Watch the video to see it in action.

Watch the video: How to set filters to ensure only relevant notifications are replicated

Discover Industrial System Associations via OData API

Understanding how technical objects relate to your industrial system is essential for transparency, integration, and advanced analytics.

To support this, the Technical Object OData API has been enhanced to expose industrial system relationships directly within the service.

What’s new

The API schema now includes two new associations:

  • Industrial System Perspective
  • Industrial System Mapping

These additions allow consumers of the API to retrieve and clearly identify how technical objects are associated within the industrial system structure.

Why this matters

With industrial system relationships now accessible through the API:

  • Integration scenarios become more transparent
  • External applications can better interpret system context
  • Reporting and analytics can incorporate industrial structure information
  • Custom extensions and automation scenarios gain richer structural insight

This enhancement improves data discoverability and makes system relationships accessible in a standardized, API-driven way. For more information, see the official documentation for the Explore Technical Objects API.

SAP Business Accelerator Hub in the Schema View of the “Explore Technical Objects” API

Industrial Systems - Doubling Capacity for Complex Systems

The challenge

As industrial environments grow, so does their complexity. Large plants, distributed equipment landscapes, and deeply structured functional locations require detailed modeling to ensure transparency, traceability, and performance management.

Until now, System Elements were limited to 1,000 Technical Objects. For smaller setups, this worked well. But in enterprise-scale environments, that ceiling quickly became a bottleneck.

The solution

We have doubled the previous assignment capability. You can now:

  • Assign up to 2000 technical objects per System Element for the Plant system perspective
  • Assign up to 2000 technical objects per System Element for Functional Location and Equipment perspective.

The result

  • Improved scalability to support large and complex industrial environments.
  • Increased customer satisfaction by supporting enterprise-scale industrial use cases.

Asset Reliability Engineering

FMEA Assessments: Now transitioned to the Extensible SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Model

The challenge

As of today, the Manage FMEA Assessments application does not support field extensibility, timeline changes, or standard sort and search capabilities across its list and object detail views. This limits flexibility and makes it difficult for organizations to tailor assessments to their specific industry or business requirements. Managing and navigating assessments can become inefficient, especially when customization or structured data handling is required.

The solution

To address these limitations, we introduced a new FMEA Assessment application based on the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) and OData APIs. The new application provides out-of-the-box support for extensibility, enabling users to add custom fields and adapt the application to industry-specific requirements and data capture needs.

During a transition period, the new application will gradually replace the current one, providing a modern and scalable foundation for assessment management.

Object page in the FMEA CAP-based application and new tile from SAP Fiori Launchpad

The result

With centralized handling and built-in extensibility, organizations gain greater flexibility and control over their FMEA assessments. The enhanced capabilities support customized data structures and industry-specific adaptations, while also delivering an improved user experience with more intuitive, consistent, and efficient assessment management.

To use this application, you must have the role FMEAAssessment_CAP assigned to your user.

For more information, refer to Manage FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) Assessments .

SAP Help Portal  

For more information on release timelines, implementation, support, and known limitations, refer to SAP Note 3603407

Define Validity Periods for FMEA assessments

You can now define validity periods for FMEA assessments

The challenge

FMEA assessments are not static documents — they evolve over time. However, without structured validity control, it can be difficult to clearly define when an assessment becomes effective or when it expires.

This lack of time-based governance can lead to confusion around which version of an assessment is currently valid, especially when assets, operating conditions, or risk evaluations change. Managing assessment lifecycle and traceability without clear validity periods reduces transparency and can impact compliance and audit readiness.

The solution

The enhanced FMEA Assessment application introduces structured validity date management. Users can define “Valid From” and “Valid To” dates directly within the assessment using an intuitive calendar control.

This enables clear lifecycle control of each assessment and ensures that time-dependent changes are properly documented. Validity dates allow organizations to manage transitions between assessment versions in a controlled and transparent way, supporting structured updates and future-dated planning.

Validity section in the FMEA assessment object page

The result

With defined validity periods, FMEA assessments become time-aware and easier to govern.

Organizations gain:

  • Clear visibility into which assessment version is currently active
  • Improved traceability of historical and future assessments
  • Better lifecycle management and compliance support

By integrating validity control directly into the application, FMEA assessments are no longer static records but governed, time-based reliability artifacts.

 

Asset Health Monitoring

Rerun Failed Rules for Technical Objects

The challenge

Rule-based automation plays a central role in maintaining data quality and process consistency across technical objects.

When a rule fails, however, automation slows down. Failed executions can lead to inconsistencies, follow-up effort, and delays in downstream processes (especially in large industrial environments managing high volumes of data).

Without a direct way to rerun failed rules, restoring process stability required additional steps and manual intervention.

The solution

The View Rule Logs application now includes a Rerun Rules button, allowing users to rerun failed rules for technical objects immediately. This enables fast recovery directly within the process with no workaround and no disruption.

Access to this capability is governed by the new Rules_Execute role:

  • Currently, the function works without explicit role assignment
  • Starting with release 2608, users must have the Rules_Execute role to use it

This ensures operational flexibility today and strengthened governance going forward.

New "Rerun Rules" button in the View Rule Logs application

The result

With this enhancement, organizations gain:

  • Faster resolution of failed rule executions
  • Reduced manual correction effort
  • Improved data consistency and reliability
  • Clear authorization control over rule re-execution

For more information, see View Rule Logs.

 

Stay Informed and Get Involved

  • If you missed them, check out our recent roadmap and What's New 2602 (incl. 2510-2601) webcasts
  • You can also explore our latest blog posts here, including topics such as Embedded IoT by @florian_weigold, Industrial Systems by @SonamKinger, and the extensibility blog by @archigarg.
  • Find out what's up and coming in the SAP Road Map Explorer.
  • For an exhaustive list of all new features, visit the SAP Help Portal - What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management.
  • Send us your improvement requests. Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session.
  • Check the January update of the APM link collection. 

See you for the 2603 update!

entry.title: Re: No Option to choose Item Category "U" - Stock Transfe...
entry.content:
Good day @saijalashok 

Thank you for your question.

That is because you are using Public Cloud. The My Purchase Requisitions app does not provide item category U or L for PR creation in public cloud. See KBA 3040361 and KBA 2564079 for more details.

If your goal is Stock Transfer, I suggest you crate a Stock Transport Order with the purchase order apps, instead of trying to crate a PR with item category U.

If Subcontracting is your aim, then create a Subcontracting Purchase Order directly with the item category L at the level or your Purchase Order.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to switch off E-Mail notifcation for flexible wor...

entry.title: Re: Embed React Components to SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi ,

I am trying to implement stacked column chart with dynamic data using third party scripts inside inline frame. My code is 

<!doctype html>
<html>
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta
      name="viewport"
      content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1,maximum-scale=1,user-scalable=no"
    />
    <title>Stacked Column (ECharts)</title>
    <style>
      html, body, #chart { height: 100%; width: 100%; margin: 0; }
      body { background: #ffffff; }
    </style>
    <!-- Apache ECharts from jsDelivr -->
    <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/echarts@5/dist/echarts.min.js"></script>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div id="chart" role="img" aria-label="Stacked column chart"></div>
    <script>
      // Parse JSON passed via URL hash
      function parseHash() {
        try {
          const raw = decodeURIComponent(location.hash.slice(1));
          return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : null;
        } catch (e) {
          console.error("Invalid hash JSON:", e);
          return null;
        }
      }

 

      // Default demo data if none supplied
      const fallback = {
        categories: ["Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun"],
        series: [
          { name: "Open", data: [120, 132, 101, 134, 90, 230, 210] },
          { name: "In Progress", data: [220, 182, 191, 234, 290, 330, 310] },
          { name: "Closed", data: [150, 232, 201, 154, 190, 330, 410] }
        ],
        yLabel: "Count",
        title: "Work Orders by Day (Stacked)"
      };

 

      const incoming = parseHash() || fallback;

 

      const chart = echarts.init(document.getElementById("chart"));

 

      function render(data) {
        const option = {
          title: data.title ? { text: data.title, left: "center" } : undefined,
          tooltip: { trigger: "axis", axisPointer: { type: "shadow" } },
          legend: { top: 28 },
          grid: { left: 40, right: 20, top: 60, bottom: 40, containLabel: true },
          xAxis: { type: "category", data: data.categories || [] },
          yAxis: { type: "value", name: data.yLabel || "" },
          series: (data.series || []).map(s => ({
            name: s.name,
            type: "bar",
            stack: "total",
            emphasis: { focus: "series" },
            data: s.data || [],
            // Optional: rounded bars
            itemStyle: { borderRadius: [3, 3, 0, 0] }
          }))
        };
        chart.setOption(option, true);
      }

 

      render(incoming);

 

      // Re-render if hash data changes
      window.addEventListener("hashchange", () => {
        const d = parseHash();
        if (d) render(d);
      });

 

      // Handle resize
      window.addEventListener("resize", () => chart.resize());
    </script>
  </body>
</html>
And enabled allow-modals allow-scripts allow-same-origin in sandbox in properties. But still I am getting error as below

Thanks and Regards 

Saranya

entry.title: Re: Account code which is used in foreign currency valuat...
entry.content: That should be possible via configuration. The accounts used by FAGL_FCV are controlled in Prepare Automatic Postings for Foreign Currency Valuation (OB09). Assignment is by valuation area + account determination key, so not directly changeable elsewhere.

entry.title: Re: Value differences are posted solely to stock account...
entry.content: That is standard behavior. MIRO checks each line item individually, so differences post to the stock account. Proportional distribution is not supported as far as I know, so only possible via custom enhancement/BAdI.

Dear SAP 

When SAP Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation job is run, it generates lots of reports as spool , we need the spool to be downloaded as is required by auditor for FX calculation verification which spool has the data but after the run it generates 100s of output report which is impossible to download every month.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to download Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation r...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

For your requirement of download large amount of data from Result list of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation, please check out the app Analyze Closing Run Results ( App ID: F3753 ).

With this app, you can display, filter, and analyze the results of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation jobs in detail. You can also use it for analysis, reporting, and reconciliation purposes. Crucially, it can be used to download all entries from the result list, or only those that you've filtered, into a spreadsheet for further analysis.
Please keep in mind that there is also a limit in Excel line item, so if your Result list contain more than 1 million line item, please consider divide it by filter condition and download them in smaller batch.
Regard.

entry.title: Cross Delivery HUs in ASR Scenario with TM
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

It is announced that cross delivery handling units are supported in ASR scenario with the SAP S/4HANA 2025 in SAP. (KBAs: https://me.sap.com/notes/3702034 and https://me.sap.com/notes/3423370) 

Is it only supported with ASR with EWM? or is it also supported with ASR with LWM (lean warehouse management, MM or IM warehouses)? Because I think this part is not clear.

And for both ASR in EWM and LWM, can unified package building (UPB and TM PB) be used to create cross delivery handling units or is it only be created manually? For me this question is matters a lot, because in the case of TU based integration, there are TU documents that consolidates deliveries, which could be considered as consolidation document to create cross delivery HUs. But in ASR such a consolidation document does not exist, if system is not checking FOs. (BTW ASR is not supporting consolidation package building aka late package building in TM.)

Thanks in advance,

Kaya
