entry.title: Using SAP Enable Now as a platform for mandatory training
entry.content:
Hello SAP Enable Now experts, we are currently evaluating our future learning and enablement architecture and we would like to understand whether SAP Enable Now can be used not only as a content creation tool, but also as a platform for delivering and managing mandatory training. Our goal is to use one solution for creating, assigning and tracking e-learning courses (for example compliance, onboarding or role-based mandatory training).

We would appreciate your guidance on the following topics.

1. SAP Enable Now as a learning delivery platform

Is it possible to use SAP Enable Now Manager as a learning platform where:

  • learners access assigned courses

  • training is role-based

  • learning content is structured into curricula or learning paths

If yes:

  • what needs to be configured?

  • what are the technical and functional prerequisites?

  • are there any limitations compared to a standard LMS?

2. Assignment of mandatory training

We need to assign mandatory courses to specific users or roles.

Is it possible in SAP Enable Now to:

  • assign training to users or user groups?

  • define that a course is mandatory?

  • set a due date for completion?

How is this managed from an administrator perspective?

3. Tracking and reporting

For audit and compliance purposes we need to track:

  • who has completed the training

  • who has not completed the training

  • completion date

  • learning progress

  • test results / pass-fail status

Can SAP Enable Now Manager provide this level of reporting?

Are there standard reports available for this scenario?

4. Notifications and reminders

Is it possible to automatically send:

  • training assignment notifications

  • reminders for incomplete training

  • escalation for overdue courses?

If yes:

  • is this handled directly in SAP Enable Now

  • or via integration with another SAP solution (e.g. SAP BTP, IAS, LMS)?

5. Recurring and re-certification training

Many mandatory trainings must be repeated regularly.

Can SAP Enable Now:

  • automatically reassign training after a defined period?

  • support recurring certification scenarios?

6. Identity management and authorizations

What is the recommended setup for:

  • user provisioning

  • role-based content assignment

  • SSO integration

in this learning scenario?

7. Licensing model

We would like to understand the licensing impact if SAP Enable Now is used as a learning delivery platform for a large number of learners.

Specifically:

  • which license type is required for learners consuming the training?

  • is there any difference if the content is accessed via Manager?

  • how should we size the licenses for this scenario?

8. Recommended architecture and best practices

From SAP’s perspective:

  • Is this a recommended scenario?

  • In which cases should SAP Enable Now be combined with an LMS?

  • Are there reference architectures or best practices available?

9. Available documentation and enablement materials

Could you please share links to:

  • official documentation

  • configuration guides

  • learning architecture recommendations

  • real customer use cases

for this scenario?

Thank you very much for your support.
Your recommendations will help us define the target enablement and learning architecture.

Kind regards,

Helena Černá

entry.title: Re: IaC for SAP BTP: Automating Environment Management with Terraform and CI/CD
entry.content:
@Atul22Joshi For CF you can use the Terraform provider for CF (https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudfoundry/cloudfoundry/latest) that is maintained by the same team as the Terraform provider fro SAP BTP. This is working well.

For Kyma or Kubernetes there is the generic Kubernetes provider (https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/kubernetes/latest). It depends if you want to go for Terraform to manage Kubernetes rersources or if you want to stick to a K8s-native tooling like helm.

entry.title: Nota Fiscal Debit Note processing requirement in case of purchase returns (Partial Refusal)
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Considering Brazil tax reforms and to follow mandatory compliance legislation, we have business requirement to issue a nota fiscal debit note in case there is supplier returns scenario with partial quantity rejection/ refusal by the recipient. At present (currently) the NFE is getting issued manually by the recipient with reference to original NFE to register.

With the mandatory compliance legislation for tax reforms in Brazil, in future the recipient (Ordering Organization) would like to issue a nota fiscal debit note in place of NFE (current process). Please help me to know if SAP has released any Brazil specific standard correction/ KBA notes that can help to address this business requirement. Appreciate your response. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Closing Month in SAC (BW Import Model)

@onursoyer 

You could explore creating a Date Input control with range option and then selecting custom Date. This will create a date picker which you can configure to month granularity. This can act as selection for the close period. Then you configure your widgets with current selection and then for the other trend chart with an offset of -12 or -13 .

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Customizing Remote Function Adapter's Component List for SAP PAPM On Premise
entry.content:
Profitability and Performance Management (PAPM) offers a list of Remote Function Adapter (RFA) types which can be used to communicate to other applications and systems. This is done by reading or by posting data, on the same system as PAPM, or on remote systems (for example, SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA). 

Each RFA type comes with a predefined list of fields, show as components on the function's Rules Tab. To these components, the user can map fields which provide input values or store output results. The RFA works as a wrapper, by calling SAP BAPIs, custom function modules or OData services, and mapping its list of components to the corresponding importing and exporting parameters. 

Until now, to extend a function's list of components in order to meet the specific needs of a user, the user would create a new feature request here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3041.

To address the users' demands for flexibility and efficiency, I will guide you on how to customise the list of RFA components, and add new fields to it. 

1. Identifying the SAP function module called by the RFA function. 

Use the table below to find which BAPI is called by your RFA type. 

RFA typeTechnical abbreviationSAP Function ModuleNote
Finance Accounts PayableFI_APBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance Accounts Payable ItemsFI_APIBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance Accounts ReceivableFI_ARBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance Accounts Receivable ItemsFI_ARIBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance ExtendedFI_EXTBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance General LedgerFI_GLBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Finance General Ledger ItemsFI_GLIBAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST 
Purchase OrderPOBAPI_PO_CREATE1Limitation: systems with version of S/4 HANA OP1610 or lower - see details below
Primary Cost RepostingPRBAPI_ACC_PRIMARY_COSTS_POSTLimitation: systems with version of S/4 HANA OP1610 or lower - see details below

Sales and Distribution

type = 'SALESORDER'

type = 'QUOTATION'

type = 'INQUIRY'

SD

 

BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2

BAPI_QUOTATION_CREATEFROMDATA2

BAPI_INQUIRY_CREATEFROMDATA2

Limitation: systems with version of S/4 HANA OP1610 or lower - see details below
Secondary Cost Element PostingSPBAPI_ACC_MANUAL_ALLOC_POST 

 

2. Identifying the function module parameter and field you need

Let's have a simple example, where the user wants to add the field 'Company ID of Trading Partner' to RFA FI_AP. 

  • On your target system, run transaction SE37.
  • Fill in function module name - BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST and click the Display button (Figure 1). 
  • Go to tab called Tables (Figure 2).
  • Double click on the associated type BAPIACGL09, corresponding to parameter name ACCOUNTGL.
  • Identify the field TRADE_ID, which has the type CHAR 6, decimals 0, and the description 'Company ID of Trading Partner' (Figure 3). Copy these field technical details.

 Figure 1

Figure 2

Figure 3

3. Maintaining the RFA customizing view

Before modifying the customizing view, we need to check that the necessary placeholder for our field already exists. This is a mandatory condition for the end user to independently add their desired fields.

A. Identifying the placeholder

  • On your local system, run transaction /N/NXI/P1_MF.
  • Navigate in the tree to 'Remote Function Placeholder'.
  • In the list, search for the Placeholder which has the same exact name as the BAPI parameter you just viewed. For our current example, this is 'ACCOUNTGL' (Figure 4).

 Figure 4

Once we make sure that we have all the necessary information, we can move forward to the final step.

B. Adding the new RFA component.

  • Navigate in the tree to 'Remote Function Adapter Mapping'. 
  • Fill in Remote Function Adapter Type with the value 'FI_AP' (code for Finance Accounts Payable), then press 'Enter'.
  • Press the 'Change' button (or Ctrl+F1) and then the 'New Entries' button (or F5).
  • Add all the necessary technical information for the field. (Figure 5)
    Component: structure_name-field_name. Each field belongs to a group of fields, which are then mapped to a BAPI structure. It helps to follow the examples of the fields already defined.
    Sequence: the order in which the components appear on the application UI, on the RFA Rules tab.
    Placeholder: must have the same technical name as the BAPI's parameter.
    Is Mandatory: when choosing Yes, the component will be colored on the application UI. It will red, when the field is not mapped to a value or an environment field, and green, once the field is mapped.
    Declaration Type: RFA components which will be passed to processing are of type Input. RFA components which represent results of processing are of type Output. 
    DDIC Data Type, Data Length, Data Decimals and Name are all technical information taken from the BAPI field definition. 
  • Press 'Save' and select an appropriate Transport Request.

 Figure 5

After activating the objects and releasing the transport requests, the newly added field can be found on the application UI.

❗Limitations

Please note this user-led customization process has the following limitations:

  1. Only available from some RFAs (listed in the table above), where the functionality is simple enough that it can easily align with the customization. 
  2. Only available for systems with version of S/4 HANA OP1610 or lower, in case of RFA PO, PR, SD. PAPM is optimized for S/4HANA 1610. If S/4HANA Version is higher than 1610, the parameters of SAP function modules may have changed data types. This would require more complex work in order to add new RFA components, not just performing the above steps. 
  3. Only available for fields of BAPI parameters which can already be found in the Placeholders list. Adding a new placeholder would require more complex work, including source code changes.

If you find that you are facing one of the above limitations, please create a new feature request for the enhancement you wish to acquire, here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3041.

 

By following these steps - identifying the SAP function module name, identifying the parameter name and field technical details, maintaining the RFA's customizing view, you now have everything you need to complete the process from start to finish. Once you implement this guide, you'll save time, gain agency and get better and faster results every time you repeat the process. Start with a field and carefully test the results of your work. 

If you run into questions, drop them in the comments and I am happy to help!

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM Health Monitoring for S/4HANA SAPConnect
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Greetings from SAP Support.

The metric "SAPConnect Send Errors (Today)" is a newly released metric. To enable data collection for this metric, a minimum version of ST-PI 740 SP33 is required on the managed system.

Please check if your SAP S/4HANA managed system meets the prerequisite.

Once your SAP S/4HANA managed system has been upgraded to ST-PI 740 SP33, the metric will become available and visible in the monitoring tile accordingly.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: BOM Should Not Create For Boughtout Material Currentl...
entry.content:
The most robust approach in Public Cloud is to use Custom Logic (Key User Extensibility):

  • Add a validation on the BOM creation app (Maintain Bill of Material).
  • If the material type = bought-out, raise an error message and stop the process.

This way, you don’t rely solely on user discipline but enforce system-level control.

entry.title: Re: Settlement Process for Lump Sum and Slotting Fee usin...
entry.content:
Best Practice Process Design

  • Centralize all trade terms under a structured framework (Condition Contract Management or Settlement Management).
  • Accruals at the time of sale: Each fee/discount should be accrued as a statistical condition in billing or sales order, ensuring visibility in margin analysis.
  • Subsequent settlement: Actual payout/credit memo should be triggered via settlement runs or manual credit memo requests.
  • Transparency in reporting: Link conditions to COPA value fields for Gross-to-Net revenue analysis.

Recommended SAP Solution Approach

1. Condition Contract Management (CCM) – Standard Approach

Use Customer Condition Contracts for rebates, listing fees, shelf rentals, and promotions. Each arrangement (account opening, listing, shelf rental, etc.) can be modeled as a condition type within CCM. Accruals are posted automatically, and settlement generates credit memo requests → credit memos.

2. Settlement Management Framework

Settlement Management in S/4HANA is designed for subsequent settlement of rebates (both customer and vendor).

  • It supports:
    • Condition contracts
    • Business volume selection criteria
    • Automated accrual postings
    • Settlement documents (credit memo requests)

3. Custom Enhancements (if needed)
For Account Opening Fee (one-time, not volume-based), you may need a custom condition type or a Fiori app extension to trigger a credit memo manually. For Other Fees (renovation, flyer, e-commerce), if they are not linked to sales volume, they can be handled as manual settlement conditions or via custom contract types.

entry.title: From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This February
entry.content:
In the age of AI, the skills we build today will define how we work tomorrow. That’s why we’ve launched a monthly blog series that spotlights the latest AI learning assets and upcoming learning opportunities.

Building on my first blog, where I introduced you to our AI live sessions Beyond Hype (which is all about getting productive with SAP Business AI), I wanted to address some of your most asked questions:

  1. What is the difference between Joule skills and Joule agents?

    Joule skills are designed for executing single, predefined tasks independently. These skills enhance SAP's default capabilities by leveraging unstructured inputs to perform specific, context-aware actions, such as data retrieval or system transactions, in a deterministic, predictable manner. Joule Skills integrates with both SAP and third-party APIs to automate and streamline operations such as ticket creation and workflow execution, all initiated through conversational interactions.

    Joule Agents are AI applications that assist business users with decision support and task automation. They use LLMs and reasoning models to determine actions and their sequence, addressing various situations autonomously. Joule Agents can autonomously execute complex multi-step business processes. They engage users to review and approve significant actions that impact the business, such as extending discounts to dissatisfied customers. User requests or specific business events, such as the creation of a new sales order or customer service ticket, can trigger Joule Agents.

    Deep dive by watching this video: What are Joule agents and skills?  

  2. How can we start from scratch with Joule Studio to create our own portfolio of Joule skills and Joule agents?

    To use Joule Studio, the SAP Build developer license (SKU 8019311) is required, and you need to install the SAP Build Process Automation service to leverage the Cloud Integration Automation service scenario for SAP Build. Please note that you need to be on the new unified SAP Build commercial model.

    And of course, you need Joule Base or Premium. Please note, Joule Studio works with any Joule brownfield license, except Joule for Consultants and Joule for Developers. To run custom Joule Skills in your production environments, you do not need AI Units. However, execution of custom Joule Agents will consume AI Units.

    To all Partners, a TDD license is available on the SAP Partner Portal! Read on to learn how to set up Joule Studio for all partner flows and entitlement levels. I am excited to share the link to the free 30-day basic trial. Request now and select workbook: Create a Joule Agent with Joule Studio!

    And lastly, here are the instructions to set up Joule Studio for all customer flows and entitlement levels.

  3. Besides building low-code and no-code agents with Joule Studio, can I also code them in Python?

    Of course, you can! Have a look at the SAP Community Blog Post by @noravonthenen  from the Developer Advocates. In her article, she shares her experience building an AI Agent with LiteLLM, CrewAI, and SAP-RPT-1, and what actually worked. You can also check out this GitHub repository and learn how to Build Your Own AI Agent-Based Solution using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI and the Generative AI Hub in AI Foundation.

However, let’s continue to focus on Joule Studio. In this three-part tutorial series, we will walk you through the complete journey of building your own custom AI agent in Joule Studio, from defining the business use case to extending its capabilities and finally bringing it to life.

Each part builds on the previous one. By the end, you won’t just understand the theory, you’ll have created a fully functional agent tailored to your scenario.

  1.  Define the use case: We start by grounding everything in a concrete scenario for Intelligent Maintenance Order Fulfillment. What problem are we solving? What outcome are we aiming for? This foundation ensures the agent is built with purpose.

     

  2. Extend the Joule Skill: Next, we move from concept to capability. You’ll learn how to extend an existing skill to support the custom requirements of your use case.

     

  3. Create the Joule Agent: Finally, we assemble everything into a working agent — connecting the extended skill and configuring the behavior so it can operate autonomously.

     

Want to dive deeper into the content? Rewatch the session and access the session material via SAP Learning Hub (subscription required) and explore your key resources to help you get started building your own Joule agents:

  • Step-by-step guidance to build custom Joule agents in Joule Studio (recording)
  • Joule Studio Goes Live: Answers to Your Top Questions covering Skills and the new Agent Builder features 
  • Best Practices for Building Agents: Key principles and practices for designing effective Joule Agents 
  • Discovery Center Mission: Get Hands-on experience in creating a Joule Agent with multiple skills and MCP
  • Since you also asked about a certification for Joule Studio, the SAP Build Developer Certification includes Joule Studio.

Hi Joule, where should I get started with the latest learning resources? *

Apart from live enablement sessions, get hands-on with our latest on-demand learning resources:

  • Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core, and Generative AI Hub.
  • Explore how to make Large Language Models truly enterprise-ready by grounding them with your own business data using Document Grounding in SAP Generative AI Hub.
  • Create your own schemas from scratch in the SAP Document AI workspace. Go ahead and try it yourself! 
  • Learn how to deploy an LLM on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad, the orchestration service, and the Python SDK.
  • Get started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python learn how the Machine Learning process develops using a Python Client for SAP HANA.
  • Get started with AI (Vector Engine, NLP, Knowledge Graph[soon]) using SAP HANA and Python, and learn how SAP HANA Cloud supports the development of generative AI applications.
  • Implement enterprise-grade AI solutions using SAP's AI services and integration with generative AI models.

 As you can see, some SAP Samples were used for an SAP CodeJam. An SAP CodeJam is a free, hands-on developer workshop organized by SAP that focuses on a specific technology topic. It’s designed for developers who want practical, guided experience — not just slides, but real coding. If you want to experience a SAP CodeJam in person, check out the upcoming events in your region and sign up!

* Powered by the machine learning technology of SAP AI Business Services, personalized recommendations are generated for employees, in one place on the Learning home page, based on employee data.

As we look ahead, we’re excited to share with you a new lineup of learning opportunities designed to help you expand your knowledge and make AI an advantage in your role:

  • March 5, 11 am CET: Accelerate your transformation to the Cloud with SAP Business AI. Discover how SAP’s AI-powered migration toolchain enables customers to accelerate their transformation with less risk and cost to the SAP Business Suite.
  • March 12, 11 am CET: Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI. Explore the pre-delivered document inbound processing use cases for SAP Cloud ERP across Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.
  • March 19, 10 am CET: All Things Business AI: Latest Insights, Updates & Live Demos. Discover current AI trends, recent updates across SAP’s AI portfolio, and see live demos showcasing how Business AI can drive efficiency and innovation in real-world business processes.

Stay tuned for more, and happy learning! 

Further Resources:

  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This October
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This November
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This December
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This January

Joule Studio SAP Business AI Joule  SAP Document AI AI Foundation #sapclouderp #handson

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - 計算の追加
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、営業利益率や累計売上高など元々データとして入っていなかった値を計算させて表示される方法についてご紹介します。

 


 目次：

  • SAP Analytics Cloudにおける計算の追加
  • ストーリーでの計算式追加（営業利益率、累計売上高の計算）［ハンズオン］

※ ベースとなるサンプルストーリーの作成手順は、はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityでご紹介しています。

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2026年3月に作成しています。）

SAP Analytics Cloudにおける計算の追加

SAP Anaytics Cloudで計算式を追加する方法は、大きく以下の2つがあります。

  • モデルでの計算式追加
    計算式の定義場所をモデルで一元管理するため、複数のストーリーでモデルを利用した場合でも数字の整合性がとれ、統制のとれた計算の定義が可能
  • ストーリーでの計算式追加
    ストーリー作成時に新しい項目を任意に追加し、レポート上で利用することが可能であり、分析要件に柔軟に対応可能

今回は後者のストーリーでの計算式追加についてご紹介していきます。
ストーリーの編集モードで作成している際に、チャートに対して計算式を定義して新しい項目を追加することができます。計算には四則演算の演算子だけでなく、IF()やABS()などの関数や、ANDや>などの条件も使用することができます。さらには、平均値や累計値、差異などを定義するための設定もご用意しています。

詳細はヘルプページをご覧ください：チャートでのカスタム計算の作成 | SAP Help Portal

 

ストーリーでの計算式追加（営業利益率、累計売上高の計算）［ハンズオン］

0. はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成で作成したストーリーを編集モードで開こう！

0.1. ナビゲーションバーのファイルより作成したストーリーを開き、「編集」をクリックします。

0.2. 「Page_1」の右横の「v」をクリックし、「複製」をクリックしてページを複製します。

 

0.3. 「Page_1 (1)」の右横の「v」をクリックし、「タイトルの編集」をクリックし、タイトルを「計算」に変更します。

 

1. 営業利益率を計算させて表示しよう！

1.1. 数値ポイントチャートをクリックした上、右パネル（ビルダパネル）を開きます。

1.2. メジャー > 一次値 > 「＋メジャーの追加」をクリックします。

1.3. 「＋計算の追加」をクリックします。

1.4. 営業利益÷売上高の計算を追加します。タイプを「計算メジャー」を選択し、名前(ID)を「営業利益率」と入力します。式の編集欄で「[」と入力し、ポップアップで出てきたメジャーの候補から「営業利益」を選択します。

1.5. 続けて「/」を入力して、もう一度「[」と入力し、ポップアップで出てきたメジャーの候補から「売上高」を選択します。

1.6. 左下の「書式」をクリックし、「有効な式です。」と表示されることを確認します。「OK」をクリックします。

1.7. 書式を変更します。追加された営業利益率の「•••」をクリックし、「書式...」をクリックします。

1.8. 「基本のメジャーの単位を使用」のチェックを外してから 、スケールを「パーセント」に変更し、「OK」をクリックします。

1.9. 営業利益率が表示されました！

 

2. 累計売上高を計算させて表示させよう！

年度単位で累計値で計算させて、四半期ごとの累計を表示させてみよう！

 2.1. 売上高の棒チャートをクリックし、ビルダパネルにてメジャー > 売上高 > 「×」をクリックしてメジャーから外します。

2.2. メジャー > 「＋少なくとも1メジャーが必要です」をクリックします。

2.3. 「＋計算の追加」をクリックします。

2.4. 売上高を年度ごとに累計させる計算を作成します。タイプを「制限メジャー」と選択し、名前(ID)を「累計売上高」と入力します。メジャーを「売上高」、ディメンションを「月」、値を「ナビゲーション > 終了日付(累計) > 年度累計」を選択して「OK」をクリックします。
2.5. ディメンションが四半期ごとに設定されているため四半期ごとの累計が表示され、年度ごとに更新されていることが確認できます。

今回は計算の追加方法の紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。
はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone: Tile Opens(content federation) but App Fails with ‘IP Address Cannot Be Found
entry.content:
In my SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition) environment, I faced an issue where a dynamic tile loaded successfully through content federation, but clicking the tile caused the application to fail with a browser error:

ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED – The IP address of the server could not be found

The failing URL always contained a very long auto‑generated hostname.

 

Interestingly, the dynamic tile itself worked correctly (e.g., OData count displayed), but navigating to the corresponding SAP Fiori app consistently failed. This clearly indicated that the issue was not with backend connectivity or destinations, but with the content runtime URL generated by Work Zone.
 
Please refer the screenshots.

entry.title: Re: For outbound delivery types I want to keep transporta...
entry.content:
Your STO outbound deliveries are being blocked because they are now flagged as transportation planning relevant before EWM can act. This behavior was likely introduced by a customizing change or a support package in February 2026. The fix is to adjust the delivery type / transportation relevance settings or use a BAdI so that EWM can continue independently.

So the Recommended approach is

1) If TM is not required for STO flows:
Mark STO deliveries as “Not relevant for transportation planning” in delivery type customizing. This bypasses TM and lets EWM process independently.

2) If TM is required for some STOs but not all:
Use a BAdI (e.g., BADI_SD_SHIPMENTS) to control transportation relevance dynamically. For STO deliveries, skip TM distribution.

3) If TM is required but you want EWM parallel processing:
Adjust the integration profile so that EWM activities (picking, packing, staging) can proceed even if TM planning is pending.

entry.title: Do you know what it takes to keep Fiori running smoothly?
entry.content:
A brand‑new Learning Journey has just landed on SAP Learning—and it’s one you won’t want to miss.
The SAP Fiori – System Administration learning journey is now live, offering a complete, structured path for everyone who wants to configure, operate, and optimize SAP Fiori landscapes like a pro.

This new Learning Journey provides:

  • Clear, structured guidance from basics to deep‑dive expertise
  • Practical, hands‑on configuration topics covering Gateway, Web Dispatcher, and app activation
  • End‑to‑end administration skills, including monitoring and troubleshooting
  • Coverage aligned with SAP’s evolving best practices for Fiori system administration

Who should explore It?

  • System administrators
  • Technical consultants
  • Anyone supporting SAP S/4HANA and Fiori landscapes
  • Learners aiming for the SAP Certified Technology Associate – SAP Fiori System Administration certification 

Explore the new learning journey here: 👉 here
